title: quercus wislizeni var. extima
intro: Quercus wislizeni var. extima, commonly known as the Interior Live Oak, is a variant of the live oak tree native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. Its bark, leaves, and acorns have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Quercus wislizeni var. extima include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and improve cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and are often used to support digestive health and boost the immune system.
